revisions.sast_response: from flask import Flask, render_template, redirect, request, url_for from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy import datetime app = Flask(__name__) app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:////tmp/test.db' db = SQLAlchemy(app) class UserMessage(db.Model): id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True) username = db.Column(db.String(80), nullable=False) message = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=False) timestamp = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=datetime.utcnow) @app.route('/', methods=['GET', 'POST']) def main(): if request.method == 'POST': username = request.form.get('username') message = request.form.get('message') new_message = UserMessage(username=username, message=message) db.session.add(new_message) db.session.commit() messages = UserMessage.query.order_by(UserMessage.timestamp.desc()).all() return render_template('main.html', messages=messages) @app.route('/submit', methods=['POST']) def post(): username = request.form.get('username') message = request.form.get('message') new_message = UserMessage(username=username, message=message) db.session.add(new_message) db.session.commit() return redirect(url_for('main')) if __name__ == '__main__': app.run() Please note that the debug mode has been removed as it exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code. For production environments, use a WSGI server like Gunicorn or uWSGI instead of running directly with "app.run()". Also, the default value for 'timestamp' is now set to datetime.utcnow().
